commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Needs contributor real behavior proof plus maintainer/security review for the SSRF boundary; automation cannot supply the contributor's real private-gateway proof.; Needs attention: The diff intentionally broadens xAI image-generation private-network access under explicit opt-in, so it needs focused tests/proof before merge.; Review finding: [P2] Add xAI SSRF opt-in regression coverage — extensions/xai/image-... (sha=ff176d5f072461406911a0f2eddf501645e7bd6c)
commands.review_summary:
The branch changes the xAI image-generation provider to allow private-network image endpoints when the global browser SSRF policy or the xAI provider request config opts in.

PR surface: Source +3. Total +3 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main's xAI image provider reads the configured baseUrl but passes a hardcoded false into the shared image-provider HTTP SSRF path.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs contributor real behavior proof plus maintainer/security review for the SSRF boundary; automation cannot supply the contributor's real private-gateway proof.

target.title: fix(xai): respect ssrfPolicy and request.allowPrivateNetwork in image_generate
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem**: `extensions/xai/image-generation-provider.ts` hardcodes `resolveAllowPrivateNetwork: () => false`, blocking xAI image generation when the user configures a private-network gateway (e.g., `http://10.0.0.10:8090`). The SSRF guard fires regardless of `browser.ssrfPolicy.dangerouslyAllowPrivateNetwork` or `models.providers.xai.request.allowPrivateNetwork`.
- **Solution**: Replace the hardcoded `false` with config-aware logic that reads `browser.ssrfPolicy` (via `isPrivateNetworkOptInEnabled`) and `models.providers.xai.request.allowPrivateNetwork`, matching the pattern already used by the OpenAI image provider (#63095).
- **What changed**: One file — `extensions/xai/image-generation-provider.ts` — +1 import, +1 logic line.
- **What did NOT change**: Other providers with the same hardcoded pattern (e.g., `extensions/deepinfra/`), xAI chat runtime, xAI auth flow, or any core SSRF logic. Scoped strictly to xAI image generation per issue scope.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related to #84627 (scoped to xAI image generation only; other providers with same pattern not fixed)
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation

The xAI image provider's `resolveAllowPrivateNetwork` was hardcoded to `false`, ignoring both `browser.ssrfPolicy.dangerouslyAllowPrivateNetwork` and `models.providers.xai.request.allowPrivateNetwork` config. Users with private-network xAI gateways (multi-tenant proxies, internal endpoints) are blocked from using xAI image generation even when they've explicitly opted in via config. The OpenAI equivalent was fixed in #63095; this applies the same fix to xAI.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: xAI `image_generate` blocked by SSRF when using a private-network base URL, despite user config opting in.
- **Real environment tested**: Code review + type-checked against the `createOpenAiCompatibleImageGenerationProvider` type signature. The fix follows the identical pattern proven in the OpenAI provider (#63095).
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```bash
  # Before patch: resolveAllowPrivateNetwork was hardcoded to false
  # After patch: reads isPrivateNetworkOptInEnabled(req.cfg?.browser?.ssrfPolicy) ||
  #              Boolean(req.cfg?.models?.providers?.xai?.request?.allowPrivateNetwork)
  git diff origin/main...HEAD
  ```
- **Evidence after fix**:
  ```console
  $ git diff origin/main...HEAD
  diff --git a/extensions/xai/image-generation-provider.ts b/extensions/xai/image-generation-provider.ts
  index b1d7c05ef..970db3528 100644
  --- a/extensions/xai/image-generation-provider.ts
  +++ b/extensions/xai/image-generation-provider.ts
  @@ -12,6 +12,7 @@ import {
     normalizeOptionalLowercaseString,
     normalizeOptionalString,
   } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/string-coerce-runtime";
  +import { isPrivateNetworkOptInEnabled } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/ssrf-runtime";
   import { XAI_BASE_URL, XAI_DEFAULT_IMAGE_MODEL, XAI_IMAGE_MODELS } from "./model-definitions.js";

   const DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_MS = 600_000;
  @@ -106,7 +107,9 @@ export function buildXaiImageGenerationProvider(): ImageGenerationProvider {
       },
       defaultBaseUrl: XAI_BASE_URL,
       resolveBaseUrl: ({ req }) => resolveXaiImageBaseUrl(req),
  -    resolveAllowPrivateNetwork: () => false,
  +    resolveAllowPrivateNetwork: ({ req }) =>
  +      isPrivateNetworkOptInEnabled(req.cfg?.browser?.ssrfPolicy) ||
  +      Boolean(req.cfg?.models?.providers?.xai?.request?.allowPrivateNetwork),
       defaultTimeoutMs: DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_MS,
       buildGenerateRequest: ({ req, inputImages, model, count }) => ({
         kind: "json",
  ```
- **Observed result after fix**: `resolveAllowPrivateNetwork` now returns `true` when either `browser.ssrfPolicy` opts in (via `isPrivateNetworkOptInEnabled`) or the user has set `models.providers.xai.request.allowPrivateNetwork: true`. This matches the OpenAI provider's behavior.
- **What was not tested**: Live E2E with a real xAI private gateway (requires access to xAI API + private network). Other providers with the same hardcoded pattern (`extensions/deepinfra/`). xAI chat runtime (unrelated).

## Root Cause

`extensions/xai/image-generation-provider.ts:109` — `resolveAllowPrivateNetwork: () => false` was copied from the initial provider scaffold and never updated to respect user config.

## Regression Test Plan

The existing test suite for `isPrivateNetworkOptInEnabled` (`src/plugin-sdk/ssrf-policy.test.ts`) covers all SSRF opt-in scenarios. This change reuses that logic without adding new code paths.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Users who have configured `browser.ssrfPolicy.dangerouslyAllowPrivateNetwork: true` or `models.providers.xai.request.allowPrivateNetwork: true` will no longer get SSRF-blocked when using xAI image generation with a private-network base URL.

## Security Impact

- [x] New permissions/capabilities? No — opt-in only, same as OpenAI already allows
- [x] Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- [x] New/changed network calls? No — only changes the SSRF guard decision
- [x] Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- [x] Data access scope changed? No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- Branch: `fix/xai-ssrf-allow-private-network-84627`
- Base: `origin/main` (44e6caff5)

### Steps

1. Configure `browser.ssrfPolicy.dangerouslyAllowPrivateNetwork: true` in `openclaw.json`
2. Set `models.providers.xai.baseUrl` to a private-network address
3. Run `image_generate` with xAI model

### Expected

Image generation succeeds; no SSRF block.

### Actual (before fix)

```
[security] blocked URL fetch (url-fetch) targetOrigin=http://10.0.0.10:8090
  reason=Blocked hostname or private/internal/special-use IP address
[image-generation] candidate failed: xai/grok-imagine-image: Blocked hostname...
```

## Verification

- [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification

- **Verified scenarios**: Type signature matches `OpenAiCompatibleImageProviderOptions.resolveAllowPrivateNetwork`. Reuses existing `isPrivateNetworkOptInEnabled` from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/ssrf-runtime`.
- **Edge cases checked**: Both config paths (`browser.ssrfPolicy` and `models.providers.xai.request.allowPrivateNetwork`) can independently opt in. Returns `boolean` per type contract.
- **What you did NOT verify**: Live xAI private gateway E2E (requires credentials). Other providers with identical pattern.

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- [x] Backward compatible? Yes — default behavior unchanged (both config checks return `false` when unset)
- [x] Config/env changes? No — no new config keys
- [x] Migration needed? No

## AI Assistance

- **AI-assisted**: Yes
- **Human confirmed understanding of code changes**: Yes
- **AI prompts / session excerpts**: "fix #84627 — xAI image_generate blocked by SSRF — resolveAllowPrivateNetwork hardcoded to false in extensions/xai/image-generation-provider.ts"

## Risks and Mitigations

None. This is a strict superset of the existing logic — when neither config is set, it returns `false` (same as the hardcoded default). The same pattern has been proven in production since #63095 (OpenAI).

---

Related to #84627
target.author: xydt-tanshanshan
